labels.name: release-note
labels.description: Denotes a PR that will be considered when it comes time to generate release notes.

labels.name: E2E/Run
labels.description: Initiate an E2E run in a PR

prs.body:
## Summary
Fixes #24454 - Channel mentions in direct messages now resolve to the correct workspace.

## Changes
- Reversed merge order in `makeGetChannelNamesMap` to prioritize post props over current team channel.

#### Release Note
```release-note
Fixes an issue where channel mentions in direct messages incorrectly resolved based on the active workspace instead of the DM's workspace context.
```

prs.title: Fix/test output readability
prs.body:
<!--
Before submitting a PR, please read https://docs.deno.com/runtime/manual/references/contributing

1. Give the PR a descriptive title.

  Examples of good title:
    - fix(std/http): Fix race condition in server
    - docs(console): Update docstrings
    - feat(doc): Handle nested reexports

  Examples of bad title:
    - fix #7123
    - update docs
    - fix bugs

2. Ensure there is a related issue and it is referenced in the PR text.
3. Ensure there are tests that cover the changes.
4. Ensure `cargo test` passes.
5. Ensure `./tools/format.js` passes without changing files.
6. Ensure `./tools/lint.js` passes.
7. Open as a draft PR if your work is still in progress. The CI won't run
   all steps, but you can add '[ci]' to a commit message to force it to.
8. If you would like to run the benchmarks on the CI, add the 'ci-bench' label.
-->

prs.title: fix(cli): improve test output readability by graying result lines (#24071)
prs.body:
This PR addresses issue #24071 by improving the readability of the test reporter output. It modifies the 
PrettyTestReporter
 to apply gray coloring to the result portion of the test output (status and timing).

As requested in the issue, this change reduces visual clutter by making the "ok" status and timing information less prominent for passing tests, allowing users to more easily scan through the list of test names.

Changes
Passing / Ignored / Cancelled Tests: The entire result line (e.g., ok (1ms)) is now printed in gray.
Failed Tests: The status label (FAILED) remains colored (red/bold) to maintain high visibility, while the timing information is grayed.
Test Steps: The same formatting logic is applied to nested test steps for consistency.
Visual Example
Before:

test name ... ok (1ms)
(All text has the same visual weight)

After:

test name ... ok (1ms)
(The ok (1ms) portion is grayed out)

Verification
Ran cargo fmt to ensure code style compliance.
Verified that the changes only affect ANSI color codes and do not alter the actual text content of the output.
Existing test snapshots should pass as they strip ANSI codes before comparison.
Fixes #24071

prs.title: feat(web): update QuotaExceededError to spec
prs.body:
Closes #30028

This PR aligns Deno with the WHATWG WebIDL update (whatwg/webidl#1465), which changes QuotaExceededError from a named DOMException entry to a dedicated subclass.

**Overview**

QuotaExceededError is now implemented as a proper DOMException subclass instead of relying on the legacy "QuotaExceededError" name. This brings the behavior in line with modern web standards while keeping backward compatibility.

**Changes**

- Removed legacy handling from 01_dom_exception.js.
- Added new QuotaExceededError class in 02_quota_exceeded_error.js.
- Included the new module in ext/web/lib.rs.
- Exposed QuotaExceededError globally.
- Updated the error builder in 99_main.js.
- Updated TypeScript definitions.

**Why :**
The spec allows two paths :
1. Simply remove "QuotaExceededError" from the DOMException names table, or
2. Also implement the subclass.
